title: artemisia tripartita var. rupicola
intro: Artemisia tripartita var. rupicola, also known as Mongolian wormwood, is a variety of the Artemisia tripartita plant, a perennial shrub native to the mountainous regions of Mongolia and China. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiparasitic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and alleviating symptoms of malaria, due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and artemisinin. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in various herbal preparations, such as capsules and ointments, to harness its medicinal properties.
